title: METHOD FOR RECORDING INFRARED ABSORPTION SPECTRA OF GASEOUS PROPANOL AND MATRIX ISOLATION METHOD
author: L.O. Meyliyev, Sh.A. Jurayeva
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article presents a detailed analysis of the experimentally recorded infrared absorption spectra of propanol in the gaseous state, determined using the Bruker IFS-120 spectrometer Fure-IQ, the absorption spectrum recording method and the matrix isolation method. To correlate the interference pattern with a certain amount of path difference in the interferometer, the radiation of the base interferogram He-Ne laser is used at the same time. Modern Fure-spectrometers have hundreds and thousands of times the signal-to-noise ratio of conventional spectral instruments. Liquid propanol with a purity of more than 99.9%, manufactured by Fluka, is used for sample preparation. In matrix isolation, the results are recorded using the IR absorption spectra of propanol.

Modern Fure-spectrometers have hundreds and thousands of times the signal-to-noise ratio of conventional spectral instruments. Liquid propanol with a purity of more than 99.9%, manufactured by Fluka, is used for sample preparation. In matrix isolation, the results are recorded using the IR absorption spectra of propanol.
